info.name: ArtistKashi API
info.description:
The **ArtistKashi API** collection covers the full backend surface of the ArtistKashi platform — an e-learning and e-commerce marketplace for artists. It contains **50 requests** organised across **10 folders**:

- **Health** — Basic and detailed health-check endpoints to verify service availability.
    
- **Authentication** — Full auth lifecycle: registration, JWT login, email verification, password reset/change, token refresh, and logout.
    
- **Users** — Retrieve, update, and delete user accounts, including the currently authenticated user and profile browsing.
    
- **Addresses** — Create, list, and delete shipping/billing addresses for a user.
    
- **Products** — Browse and retrieve individual products available on the platform.
    
- **Courses** — List and retrieve artist courses offered through the marketplace.
    
- **Cart** — Add products or courses to the cart, update quantities, and remove items.
    
- **Wishlist** — Save products or courses to a personal wishlist and remove saved items.
    
- **Reviews** — Submit, retrieve, and list public reviews for products and courses.
    
- **Admin** — Privileged endpoints for managing users, products, courses, reviews, platform configuration, and viewing dashboard stats.
    

All requests are authenticated via a **Bearer token** (`{{ACCESS_TOKEN}}`), and the base URL is controlled through the `{{BASE_URL}}` variable. A collection-level test asserts that every response returns a status code below 500.

item.name: Health

request.description: Performs a basic health check on the API server. Returns a simple status response indicating whether the service is up and reachable. Useful for uptime monitoring and load balancer probes.

Returns a detailed health report for the API, including the status of dependent services such as the database and cache. Use this for in-depth diagnostics when a basic health check indicates a potential issue.

item.description: Contains endpoints for monitoring the operational status of the ArtistKashi API. Use these requests to verify that the service is up and running, and to retrieve detailed diagnostic information about the system's health.

item.name: Authentication

request.description: Creates a new user account on the ArtistKashi platform. Accepts user details such as name, email, and password in the request body. On success, returns the newly created user object. A verification email is typically sent after registration.

Authenticates a user with their email and password and returns a JWT access token. The returned token should be stored in the ACCESS_TOKEN environment variable and used as the Bearer token for all subsequent authenticated requests.

Authenticates a user with their email and password and returns a JWT access token. The returned token should be stored in the ACCESS_TOKEN environment variable and used as the Bearer token for all subsequent authenticated requests.
Invalidates the current user's session and access token on the server side. Call this endpoint when the user explicitly signs out to ensure the token can no longer be used.
Triggers the server to send a new email verification token to the authenticated user's registered email address. Use this if the original verification email was not received or has expired.
Confirms a user's email address using the verification token sent to their inbox. Submit the token in the request body to mark the account as verified and unlock full platform access.
Initiates the password reset flow by sending a reset link or token to the provided email address. The user does not need to be logged in to call this endpoint.
Completes the password reset flow. Accepts the reset token (received via email) and the new password. On success, the user's password is updated and they can log in with the new credentials.
Allows an authenticated user to change their current password. Requires the existing password for verification along with the new desired password. The user must be logged in to call this endpoint.

exec: if (value !== undefined && value !== null) pm.collectionVariables.set("USER_ID", value);

request.description: Issues a new JWT access token using a valid refresh token. Use this endpoint to silently renew the user's session before the current access token expires, avoiding the need to re-authenticate.

item.description: Covers the complete authentication lifecycle for the ArtistKashi platform. Includes user registration, JWT-based login, email verification, password reset and change flows, token refresh, and logout. Most other endpoints require a valid Bearer token obtained from the login endpoint.

item.name: Users

request.description: Returns the full profile and account details of the currently authenticated user. Useful for populating user dashboards or verifying the identity of the logged-in session.
Updates the profile or account details of the currently authenticated user. Accepts a partial payload (PATCH), so only the fields included in the request body will be modified.
Retrieves the account details of a specific user identified by their USER_ID path parameter. Typically used by admins or for profile lookups.
Updates the account details of a specific user identified by their USER_ID. Accepts a partial payload (PATCH). Typically used by admins to modify user information.
Permanently deletes the user account identified by USER_ID. This action is irreversible. Typically restricted to admin users or the account owner.
Returns a paginated list of public artist profiles. Supports a search query parameter (q) to filter profiles by name or keyword. Useful for the platform's artist discovery and search features.

item.description: Provides endpoints for managing user accounts and profiles. Supports retrieving the currently authenticated user, looking up users by ID, updating account details, deleting accounts, and browsing public artist profiles.

item.name: Addresses

exec: if (value !== undefined && value !== null) pm.collectionVariables.set("ADDRESS_ID", value);

request.description: Creates a new shipping or billing address for the authenticated user. Include address fields such as street, city, state, postal code, and country in the request body. Returns the newly created address on success.
Returns a paginated list of all addresses saved to the authenticated user's account. Supports page and page_size query parameters. Use this to display saved addresses during checkout.
Permanently deletes a specific address identified by ADDRESS_ID from the authenticated user's account. This action cannot be undone.

item.description: Manages shipping and billing addresses associated with a user account. Supports creating new addresses, listing all saved addresses with pagination, and deleting an address by ID.

item.name: Products

exec: if (value !== undefined && value !== null) pm.collectionVariables.set("PRODUCT_ID", value);

request.description: Returns a paginated list of all products available in the ArtistKashi marketplace. Supports page and page_size query parameters. Use this to populate product listing and browse pages.
Retrieves the full details of a specific product identified by PRODUCT_ID, including its name, description, pricing, images, and availability. Use this to populate a product detail page.

item.description: Exposes public-facing product catalogue endpoints. Allows clients to browse the full list of available products with pagination and retrieve detailed information about a specific product by its ID.

item.name: Courses

exec: if (value !== undefined && value !== null) pm.collectionVariables.set("COURSE_ID", value);

request.description: Returns a paginated list of all courses available on the ArtistKashi platform. Supports page and page_size query parameters. Use this to populate course listing and browse pages.
Retrieves the full details of a specific course identified by COURSE_ID, including its title, description, instructor, curriculum, and pricing. Use this to populate a course detail page.

item.description: Exposes public-facing course catalogue endpoints. Allows clients to browse all available artist courses with pagination and retrieve the full details of a specific course by its ID.

item.name: Cart

request.description: Returns the current contents of the authenticated user's shopping cart, including all added products and courses along with their quantities and pricing details.
Adds a specific product to the authenticated user's shopping cart. Include the product ID and desired quantity in the request body. Returns the updated cart entry on success.
Adds a specific course to the authenticated user's shopping cart. Include the course ID in the request body. Returns the updated cart entry on success.
Updates the quantity or other attributes of an existing cart item identified by CART_ITEM_ID. Accepts a partial payload (PATCH). Use this to change the quantity of a product already in the cart.
Removes a specific item from the authenticated user's cart using the CART_ITEM_ID path parameter. The item is permanently deleted from the cart.

item.description: Manages the authenticated user's shopping cart. Supports adding products or courses to the cart, retrieving the current cart contents, updating item quantities, and removing individual items.

item.name: Wishlist

request.description: Returns all items currently saved in the authenticated user's wishlist, including both products and courses. Use this to display the user's saved items on their wishlist page.
Saves a specific course to the authenticated user's wishlist. Include the course ID in the request body. Returns the newly created wishlist entry on success.
Saves a specific product to the authenticated user's wishlist. Include the product ID in the request body. Returns the newly created wishlist entry on success.
Removes a specific item from the authenticated user's wishlist using the WISHLIST_ITEM_ID path parameter. The item is permanently removed from the wishlist.

item.description: Manages the authenticated user's wishlist. Allows users to save products or courses for later, view all saved wishlist items, and remove items they no longer want to keep.

item.name: Reviews

request.description: Submits a new review for a product or course. Include the entity type, entity ID, rating, and review text in the request body. The authenticated user is recorded as the review author.

exec: if (value !== undefined && value !== null) pm.collectionVariables.set("REVIEW_ID", value);

request.description: Returns all reviews submitted by the currently authenticated user across all products and courses. Useful for displaying a user's review history on their profile page.

Returns a paginated list of publicly visible reviews for a specific product or course. Filter by review_type (product or course) and entity_id. Supports skip and limit query parameters for pagination.

item.description: Handles product and course reviews submitted by users. Supports creating a new review, retrieving the authenticated user's own reviews, and listing publicly visible reviews for a specific product or course.

request.description: Returns a high-level overview of platform activity for the admin dashboard, including key metrics such as total users, orders, revenue, and recent activity summaries.
Returns detailed platform statistics for administrators, including breakdowns of user growth, sales performance, course enrollments, and product metrics over time.
Returns a paginated list of all registered users on the platform, accessible only to admins. Use this to manage user accounts, search for specific users, or audit account activity.
Retrieves the full account details of a specific user identified by ADMIN_USER_ID. Admin-only endpoint used for user management and support workflows.
Returns a full list of all products on the platform from an admin perspective, including unpublished or inactive items not visible in the public catalogue. Use this for product management.
Retrieves the complete details of a specific product by PRODUCT_ID from the admin perspective, including internal fields such as status, inventory, and audit metadata.
Returns a full list of all courses on the platform from an admin perspective, including draft or unpublished courses not visible in the public catalogue. Use this for course management.
Retrieves the complete details of a specific course by COURSE_ID from the admin perspective, including internal fields such as publication status, enrollment count, and audit metadata.

Returns a paginated list of reviews from the admin perspective. Supports filtering by review_type (product or course) and status (e.g. pending, approved, rejected). Use this for review moderation workflows.
Retrieves the full details of a specific review identified by REVIEW_ID, including its content, rating, author, and current moderation status. Admin-only endpoint.
Updates the content or moderation status of a specific review identified by REVIEW_ID. Use this to approve, reject, or edit a review as part of the admin moderation workflow.
Permanently deletes a specific review identified by REVIEW_ID from the platform. Admin-only action used to remove inappropriate or spam reviews. This action cannot be undone.
Retrieves the current configuration for the platform's home page, including featured sections, banners, and promotional content settings. Admin-only endpoint.
Updates the home page configuration for the ArtistKashi platform. Accepts a full replacement payload (PUT) with the new layout, featured items, banners, and promotional content. Admin-only endpoint.

item.description: Privileged endpoints restricted to platform administrators. Covers user management, product and course administration, review moderation, dashboard statistics, admin-level stats, and home page configuration. All requests in this folder require admin-level authorization.
